commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review should wait on after-fix real behavior proof and the approval-boundary decision; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain or credential regression was found; the metadata relaxation stays inside paired-device auth and keeps device-family mismatch approval-bound. commands.review_summary:
The PR extends paired-device platform-version refresh handling so `openclaw-macos` can refresh same-family `macos ...` platform labels without a metadata-upgrade approval, and adds table cases for that behavior.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +2. Total +5 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence current-main live reproduction was run in this read-only review. Source inspection shows current main still classifies `openclaw-macos` platform-version drift as a platform mismatch, and the PR body supplies before-fix v2026.5.28 logs.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should wait on after-fix real behavior proof and the approval-boundary decision; there is no narrow code defect for ClawSweeper to repair.

target.title: fix(gateway): allow macOS app platform version refresh without re-pairing
target.body:
## Summary

- macOS app enters infinite "Approve this Mac app to control OpenClaw" dialog loop after an OS update (e.g. macOS 26.5.0 → 26.5.1)
- Root cause: `resolvePinnedClientMetadata` treats any macOS platform version change as a `platformMismatch`, triggering a repair pairing request. The pending request has a 5-minute TTL, and when it expires before the user clicks Approve, the gateway returns "unknown requestId", causing the Mac app to send a new repair request — infinite loop.
- iOS and Android already had silent platform version refresh via `normalizeMobileAppPlatformPin` — this extends the same pattern to the macOS native app (`openclaw-macos` client ID) by recognizing `"macos X.Y.Z"` as the same platform family regardless of version number.
- Renames `normalizeMobileAppPlatformPin` → `normalizeNativeAppPlatformPin` and `isMobileAppPlatformVersionRefresh` → `isNativeAppPlatformVersionRefresh` to reflect the broader scope.

## Real behavior proof

Reproduced on a real macOS 26.5.1 machine with OpenClaw 2026.5.28 gateway. After OS update from 26.5.0 → 26.5.1:

**Before fix:** Gateway log showed continuous `device.pair.approve` failures with `errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST errorMessage=unknown requestId` — the Mac app popped the approval dialog every few seconds in an infinite loop. Manual `openclaw devices approve` via CLI was required to break the cycle.

```
2026-06-03T10:26:15 [ws] ⇄ res ✗ device.pair.approve 3ms errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST errorMessage=unknown requestId
2026-06-03T10:26:16 [ws] ⇄ res ✗ device.pair.approve 4ms errorCode=INVALID_REQUEST errorMessage=unknown requestId
... (repeats indefinitely)
```

**After fix:** The `resolvePinnedClientMetadata` function returns `platformMismatch: false` and `refreshPairedPlatform: "macos 26.5.1"` for macOS version-only changes, so the paired record is silently updated on reconnect without requiring user approval — matching the existing iOS/Android behavior.

**Test output (all passing):**
```
✓ allows openclaw-macos platform version refresh without metadata-upgrade approval (macos 26.5.1 vs macos 26.5.0)
✓ allows openclaw-macos platform version refresh without metadata-upgrade approval (macos 27.0.0 vs macos 26.5.1)
✓ keeps non-native-app platform version changes approval-bound (node-host linux)
```

## What was not tested

- Did not test with a completely different platform prefix (e.g. a device claiming "windows" with clientId `openclaw-macos`) — this would correctly NOT match and still require approval.
- Integration testing with the actual Mac app binary was not performed (requires rebuilding the Swift app); the fix is server-side only.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests pass: `pnpm test` on `message-handler.post-connect-health.test.ts` (18/18 passing)
- [x] TypeScript type check passes: `tsc --noEmit`
- [x] New test cases cover macOS minor and major version bumps
- [x] Existing iOS/Android/node-host tests unaffected
- [ ] Verify with actual Mac app after next gateway release

---

🤖 AI-assisted (Claude Code). I understand what this code does — it's a straightforward extension of the existing mobile platform version refresh pattern to cover macOS, which was previously omitted.
target.author: eaveluo
